elements.grid_ref: near grid1, beside ซักล้าง (+0.50), at the gridF edge of the building — local detail-box axis labels '1'/'2' (bottom) and '6' (left side) used on this sheet, not confidently tied to the main building grid
elements.material: ลูกตั้ง/ลูกนอนบันได ผิวปูกระเบื้องเซรามิค 8"x8", จมูกบันได อลูมิเนียมสีเงิน ร่อง PVC ขนาด 2"

elements.description: Section view shows the stair descending from เฉลียง/ซักล้าง (+0.50) down to grade (ดินถม ±0.00) via 3 equal (EQ.) risers totaling 0.50m

warnings: ST.2 connects the ซักล้าง (+0.50) area down to ground level (ดินถม ±0.00) at the building's north-west corner — a full 0.50m rise via 3 equal risers, taller than ST.1's shallow 0.30m porch step.

GRID-RELEVANT: this detail box's left-side axis circle is labeled '6', which does not correspond to any named x_line or y_line in the master grid (x only goes 1-4, y only goes F-A). This does NOT appear to be a genuine structural dummy-grid candidate — no printed dimension on this sheet extends the main building footprint beyond grid1 or gridF; the '6' reads as a local/internal reference label for this detail box only (common in Thai architectural detail sheets that reuse small numbered axis circles purely for callout purposes within an isolated detail, independent of the building-wide grid). Reporting per instructions rather than silently ignoring, but NOT recommending a dummy grid line be added to the master file for this.
Independent 2-riser stair (ST.1, หน้า16) and this 3-riser stair (ST.2) are genuinely different designs serving different level transitions (0.30m vs 0.50m total rise) — not duplicates.
